---
format:
  pdf:
    lang: en
    toc: false
    number-sections: false
    keep-tex: false
    pdf-engine: xelatex
    mainfont: "Arial"
    fontenc: "T1"
    inputenc: "utf8"
    fontsize: 10pt
    title-block-style: none
    colorlinks: true
    urlcolor: blue
    linkcolor: blue
    geometry: "top=1.5cm, bottom=2cm, left=2cm, right=2cm"
    papersize: letter
    microtype: true
    linestretch: 1.0
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{xcolor}
        \usepackage[normalem]{ulem}
        \newcommand{\cvsection}[1]{%
          \vspace{-1.5em}
          \section*{\fontsize{11}{13}\selectfont\underline{\makebox[\linewidth][l]{#1}}}%
          \vspace{-1.3em}
        }
        \usepackage{setspace}
        \singlespacing
        \usepackage{enumitem}
        \setlist{itemsep=2pt, parsep=0pt, topsep=0pt, partopsep=0pt}
---

```{=latex}
\begin{center}
  {\large \textbf{Luis E. Mart\'{i}nez-Rodr\'{i}guez, Ph.D.}}\\[0.5ex]
  \normalsize
  Curriculum Vitae \\[0.5ex]
  \href{https://linkedin.com/in/luis-martinez-1394b619a}{linkedin.com/in/luis-martinez-1394b619a} \\
  luised94@mit.edu \,\textbar\, \href{https://github.com/luised94}{github.com/luised94}
\end{center}

\cvsection{Researcher Profile}

\begin{center}
Protein Purification | Genetic Engineering | NGS Analysis
\end{center}
\vspace{-1.3em}

Postdoctoral researcher combining yeast genetics, multi-protein reconstituted biochemistry, and computational genomics (ChIP-seq, WGS). Specialization in eukaryotic DNA replication initiation mechanisms. Track record of end-to-end projects spanning genetic screen design through biochemical characterization through genome-wide analysis.

\cvsection{Core Skills/Competencies}

Biochemistry: \textbf{protein purification from bacteria and fungi (multi-subunit complexes, 5+ proteins)}, electrophoretic-mobility shift assay, radioactivity-based ATPase assay, fluorescence polarization, magnetic-bead based biochemical assays, \textbf{reconstituted eukaryotic DNA-replication initiation and elongation assays}

Yeast Genetics: \textbf{genetic screen design and validation}, tetrad dissections, bulk-segregant analysis, transformations and endogenous tagging using PCR- and \textbf{CRISPR-based genome editing}, drug-sensitivity assays, flow-cytometry analysis, \textbf{auxin-inducible degron (AID2) conditional depletion}

Bioinformatics: \textbf{ChIP-seq analysis (sample preparation through peak calling and motif analysis)}, whole-genome sequencing analysis (alignment, variant calling), multiple-sequence alignment, track visualization, PyMOL

Programming: \textbf{R (genomic data visualization and analysis with Gviz, quantitative figure generation)}, \textbf{Bash (NGS pipeline development on Linux HPC: Bowtie2, SAMtools, deeptools)}, Python (variant calling), Git/GitHub, ImageJ

Cell Biology: mammalian cell line culture, electroporation of CRISPR-Cas9-gRNA ribonucleoprotein complexes, \textbf{Beacon Optofluidic System}, high-throughput immunofluorescent screening

Molecular biology: primer design, cloning, PCR, RT-qPCR, DNA and RNA extraction for next-generation sequencing, CRISPR-Cas9 guide design, SDS-PAGE and immunoblotting, site-directed mutagenesis, Gibson assembly

\cvsection{Experience}

\uline{MASSACHUSETTS INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY (MIT)} Cambridge, MA
\vspace{-0.1em}

\textbf{Post-doctoral researcher} (2023--present)
Advisor: Stephen P. Bell

\textit{In vivo} characterization of ATPase-deficient origin recognition complex allele and its suppressors using chromatin immunoprecipitation.

\begin{itemize}
\item Designed and executed ChIP-seq experiments to characterize genomic occupancy of ATPase-deficient ORC and suppressor mutants
\item Developed computational pipeline for ChIP-seq analysis: read alignment with contamination filtering, signal normalization, peak calling, and motif analysis using Bash on Linux HPC
\item Built genome-wide binding data visualization and statistical analysis workflows using R
\item Cloned and tested a panel of loss-of-function alleles in general transcription factors to evaluate suppression of the \textit{orc4-R267A} lethal phenotype
\item Implemented auxin-inducible degron (AID2) system for conditional depletion of essential replication factors
\end{itemize}

\textbf{Graduate student researcher} (2016--2023)
Advisor: Stephen P. Bell

Use of genetic and biochemical assays to understand the function of ATP hydrolysis by the origin-recognition complex during \textit{Saccharomyces cerevisiae} DNA replication.

\begin{itemize}
\item Designed and validated a genetic screen for suppressors of a lethal allele of the \textit{ORC4} gene, including analysis of whole-genome sequencing data
\item Characterized suppressor alleles genetically through CRISPR-Cas9 engineering, allele-specificity tests, and drug-sensitivity experiments
\item Characterized an ATPase-deficient mutant ORC protein biochemically, with and without suppressor mutations, using reconstituted replication assays
\end{itemize}

\textbf{Research intern, MIT/Pfizer Internship Program} (Course 7.930[J]) \hfill 2022

\textit{Development of a CRISPR-based screening platform to identify therapeutic targets for cystic fibrosis using primary human cells.}

\begin{itemize}
\item Optimized primary cell culture conditions for collagen-producing cells relevant to cystic fibrosis therapeutic target identification
\item Developed high-throughput immunofluorescent CRISPR-Cas9 screening assay using the Beacon Optofluidic System
\item Electroporated CRISPR-Cas9-gRNA ribonucleoprotein complexes into primary human cells
\end{itemize}

\cvsection{Additional Research Experience}

\uline{MASSACHUSETTS INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY (MIT)}, Cambridge, MA

Undergraduate summer research assistant (2015)

- Characterized Bfl1-selective PUMA-like peptides derived from yeast surface display using fluorescence polarization

\uline{UNIVERSITY OF NORTH CAROLINA AT CHAPEL HILL (UNC)}, Chapel Hill, NC

Undergraduate summer research assistant (2014)

- Characterized minimal aa-tRNA synthetase ATPase domains derived from opposite strands of a gene

\uline{UNIVERSITY OF PUERTO RICO-RIO PIEDRAS (UPR-RP)}, San Juan, PR

Undergraduate research assistant (2012--2015)

- Lipidic sponge phase crystallization of the nicotinic acetylcholine receptor derived from \textit{Torpedo californica}

\cvsection{Education}

Ph.D. in Biology, \uline{MASSACHUSETTS INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY (MIT)}, Cambridge, MA, 2023
Advisor: Stephen P. Bell

- Thesis: Leveraging Genetic and Biochemical Approaches to Understand the Role of ORC ATP Hydrolysis in \textit{Saccharomyces cerevisiae}

B.Sc. in Cellular and Molecular Biology, \uline{UNIVERSITY OF PUERTO RICO-RIO PIEDRAS (UPR-RP)}, San Juan, PR, 2016

\cvsection{Publications}

\textbf{Mart\'{i}nez-Rodr\'{i}guez, L.} \& Bell, S. P. (2026). A lethal ORC ATPase mutation is suppressed by alterations in ORC subunits and RNA Pol II transcription components. \textit{Submitted.}

\textbf{Mart\'{i}nez-Rodr\'{i}guez, L.}, Erdogan, O., Jimenez-Rodriguez, M., Gonzalez-Rivera, K., Williams, T., Li, L., \& Carter, C. W. (2015). Functional Class I and II Amino Acid-activating Enzymes Can Be Coded by Opposite Strands of the Same Gene. \textit{Journal of Biological Chemistry}, 290(32), 19710--19725. http://doi.org/10.1074/jbc.M115.642876

\cvsection{Presentations}
Yeast Genetics Meeting, Pacific Grove, CA. Poster Presentation. June 2026

New England Biolabs, Unorthodox Genomes Meeting. Poster presentation. March 2026.

Cold Spring Harbor Laboratory, Eukaryotic DNA Replication \& Genome Maintenance. September 2025.

Department of Biology Building 68 Annual Summer Retreat. Oral presentation. Plymouth, MA, June 2021.

Cold Spring Harbor Laboratory, Single Biomolecules. Poster presentation. September 2018.

MIT Cell Biology Supergroup. Oral presentation. Cambridge, MA, March 2018.

\cvsection{Teaching Experience}

\uline{MASSACHUSETTS INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY (MIT)}, Cambridge, MA

Teaching assistant -- Introduction to Biology (7.012, 2017) and General Biochemistry (7.05, 2020): Biweekly recitation sessions, graded assignments and exams.
```
